confidence_note: Useful as a model variant but upper tail is implausibly broad for planning.

transform_note: Classified as civilian because underlying sampled populations are IDPs and affected residents.

excerpt: September 2003 IDPs: 250,000.
confidence_note: Secondary citation to DHP No. 1; used as a start-point denominator anchor.

excerpt: MSF reported one million people affected by the war in January 2004.
transform_note: The embedded 3,000 deaths figure is mixed/unclear and not used for core DR; only the affected-population figure is used.
confidence_note: Contemporaneous field snapshot; useful midpoint anchor for episode 2 civilian exposure.

excerpt: March 2004 displaced exceeded one million.
transform_note: Used as a floor anchor rather than an exact count.
confidence_note: The source gives a threshold, not a precise point.

excerpt: October 2004 conflict-affected population: at least 2 million.
transform_note: Accessible/assessed areas only; this is not the whole region.
confidence_note: Strong denominator anchor for episode 3, but still coverage-limited.

excerpt: November 2004 conflict-affected population: 2.27 million; IDPs 1.65 million; affected residents 627,000.
transform_note: Pre-conflict 6.3 million is context only and intentionally not used as the denominator.
confidence_note: Best late-2004 anchor for episode 3 civilian exposure.

excerpt: Hilal reportedly could muster 20,000 men; 5,000-6,000 based at Misteriha.
transform_note: Used as a ceiling-type anchor for government-aligned irregular manpower, not as simultaneous theater deployment.
confidence_note: Likely includes non-simultaneously active militia and should not be read as monthly active strength.

excerpt: Early JEM size around March 2003 was no more than 100 fighters.
transform_note: Used as a lower-bound anchor for early rebel manpower, not as later-episode force size.
confidence_note: Demonstrates how small one major rebel organization was at the start.

excerpt: After late-2003 setbacks, about 500 SLA men remained in Dar Zaghawa.
transform_note: Local remnant count only; not a total SLA size.
confidence_note: Used to show how battered the SLA had become at the start of 2004.

excerpt: SLA regained some strength and was estimated around 10,000 fighters in 2004-05.
transform_note: Treated as a mid-to-late-2004 rebel strength anchor for episode 3.
confidence_note: Approximate force size; not monthly deployment.

excerpt: MSF describes significant regular-army reinforcement in early 2004.
transform_note: Qualitative input only; combined with militia and rebel counts to construct source-based combatant exposure scenarios.
confidence_note: Important for not treating Janjaweed strength as the whole combatant denominator.

excerpt: The April 2003 airport attack killed seventy troops.
transform_note: Single-event combatant death anchor showing that combatant direct deaths were real and non-trivial, though no aggregate episode-wide series was recovered.
confidence_note: Useful anchor, not an aggregate estimate.

excerpt: Pre-displacement mortality in surveyed West Darfur sites was overwhelmingly violent and civilian.
transform_note: Contextual subgroup/violence-share claim only; not rolled into region-wide death counts.
confidence_note: Supports the civilian and direct-violence interpretation of early Darfur mortality.

excerpt: GAO records a widely cited mixed-mortality estimate by State for March 2003-January 2005.
transform_note: Excluded from the core direct-death path because it mixes violence, disease, and malnutrition.
confidence_note: Retained only to document disagreement among whole-crisis estimates.

excerpt: Illustrative current-episode civilian mass-killing claim; not a theater-wide estimate.
transform_note: Used only to justify separate modern episodeization, not as a full-episode numerator.
confidence_note: Shows the 2023-present stage is materially distinct and still active.
